evidence.text: LTL tons (in thousands)2,6722,5982.8%5,3254,9308.0% LTL shipments (in thousands)3,3983,3072.8%6,7386,2118.5%

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows significant increases in LTL tons and shipments, indicating strong demand growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 22.6% and 20.0% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, as compared to the same periods in 2021.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue per hundredweight increased by over 20% in both the second quarter and first six months of 2022, showing strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Revenue increased $348.0 million, or 26.4%, and $718.8 million, or 29.4%, in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, as compared to the same periods of 2021, due to increases in both our LTL revenue per hundredweight and LTL tonnage.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth accelerated to 26.4% and 29.4% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, driven by both price and volume increases.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: LTL shipments increased by 2.8% and 8.5% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, indicating growing unit volumes.

factors.summary: The company's largest end markets are strengthening.

evidence.text: More than 98% of our revenue has historically been derived from transporting LTL shipments for our customers, whose demand for our services is generally tied to industrial production and the overall health of the U.S. domestic economy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's largest end markets are tied to strong economic indicators, suggesting a positive momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly diesel fuel costs.

evidence.text: Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel increased 99.8% and 83.6% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, as compared to the same periods last year.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Diesel fuel costs increased by 99.8% and 83.6% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, indicating significant cost pressure.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising due to increased wages and benefits.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits increased $94.2 million, or 15.4%, in the second quarter of 2022 as compared to the second quarter of 2021, due to a $62.3 million increase in salaries and wages and a $31.9 million increase in employee benefit costs.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs increased by 15.4% in the second quarter of 2022, driven by higher wages and benefits, showing significant cost pressure.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio improved to 69.5% and 71.1% for the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, from 72.3% and 74.0% in the same periods of 2021.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating ratio improved significantly, indicating expanding margins and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Our productive labor costs, which include wages for drivers, platform employees, and fleet technicians, improved as a percent of revenue to 22.0% and 23.1% in the second quarter and first half of 2022, respectively, from 24.8% and 25.5% from the same periods of 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates improving productivity, supported by a decrease in labor costs as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: Our average number of active full-time employees increased 15.1% and 16.8% for the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, as we hired additional employees to balance our workforce with higher demand and shipment trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows an increase in active employees, indicating higher capacity utilization to meet demand.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, hiring additional employees.

evidence.text: We hired additional employees to balance our workforce with higher demand and shipment trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively hiring to support growth, indicating a positive restructuring trend.

evidence.text: We believe our focus on obtaining an appropriate yield is necessary to offset rising operating costs and also allows us to invest in opportunities that can improve the quality of our service and provide capacity for future growth.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The summary suggests stable labor relations, but there is no clear indication of positive or negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Management is confident, investing in growth.

evidence.text: We believe our yield management process focused on individual account profitability, and ongoing improvements in operating efficiencies, are both key components of our ability to produce profitable growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident in their strategy, as evidenced by their focus on profitability and efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include rising fuel costs and labor expenses.

evidence.text: Our diesel fuel costs, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both the average price per gallon and consumption.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary highlights rising fuel and labor costs as near-term risks, supported by evidence of significant fuel cost increases.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is clear and well-resourced.

evidence.text: We regularly upgrade our technological capabilities to improve our customer service and lower our operating costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a clear and well-resourced long-term strategy focused on technological upgrades for growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities are diesel fuel prices and labor costs.

evidence.text: Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel increased 99.8% and 83.6% in the second quarter and first six months of 2022, respectively, as compared to the same periods last year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary identifies diesel fuel prices and labor costs as key sensitivities, with evidence of significant fuel cost increases.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new service offerings and yield management.

evidence.text: In addition to our core LTL services, we offer a range of value-added services including container drayage, truckload brokerage and supply chain consulting.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The introduction of new value-added services indicates growth and strategic expansion.
